channel.title: Daily Feed (Updated 9 AM BD)
channel.description: Daily Feed (Updated 9 AM BD) generated by script

item.title: Cancer cases could nearly double by 2050 without urgent action, WHO warns
item.description: The number of new cancer cases worldwide could rise to almost 35 million a year by 2050 unless countries take urgent action to strengthen prevention, early diagnosis and treatment, the World Health Organization (WHO) warned on Wednesday.

item.title: Grim homecoming: Devastation greets Lebanon's war-weary returnees
item.description: The ceasefire deal between Beirut and Tel Aviv has led to a marked reduction in hostilities between Hezbollah and the Israeli military sparked by the wider Iran-Israel-US war, but the situation remains uncertain for communities returning home only to find widespread destruction, UN peacekeepers said on Wednesday.

item.title: Sudan: ‘El Obeid must not become the next crime scene’ warn UN investigators
item.description: Atrocities committed by the Rapid Support Forces (RSF) militia against Sudanese civilians in El Fasher in October – including mass killings, abductions and gang rapes – bear the markers of genocide, independent UN human rights investigators reported on Wednesday.

item.title: Security Council LIVE: Open debate on sexual violence in conflict
item.description: The UN Security Council is holding an open debate Tuesday honouring the promise of international law to survivors of sexual violence in conflict as more reports emerge about warring parties using rape as a weapon, actions that constitute war crimes. Stay tuned for live updates from our meetings coverage team.

item.title: ‘Help is coming’: UN relief chief briefs on Venezuela quake recovery
item.description: The widespread solidarity shown to Venezuela must now be transformed into real, practical support for recovery from the deadly double earthquakes last month, UN relief chief Tom Fletcher said on Wednesday.

item.title: US-Iran war: Renewed attacks in Strait of Hormuz prompts another global energy alert
item.description: Renewed attacks on shipping in the Strait of Hormuz unsettled energy markets on Wednesday and prompted calls from the UN maritime agency, IMO, for “maximum restraint and de-escalation”.

item.title: UN Syria inquiry presses for answers on missing detainees after wide-ranging visit
item.description: UN investigators have urged Syria’s Government to trace thousands of missing detainees and hold perpetrators to account after a week-long visit that took in bomb attacks in Damascus, prison visits in the northeast and reports of vigilante violence in Homs.

item.title: Restoring dignity: Senegalese surgeon and Burundian foundation win reproductive health awards
item.description: A Senegalese surgeon who has dedicated his career to restoring the health and dignity of women living with obstetric fistula, and a Burundian foundation supporting vulnerable women and children, have been named the winners of the 2026 United Nations Population Award.

item.title: Roma Need Special Consideration After Ukraine War is Over
item.description: Governments, donors, NGOs, development banks and businesses recently gathered in Gdansk, Poland, to discuss reconstruction in Ukraine even as Russia’s full-scale invasion continues. But while billions of euros have been pledged for the country’s recovery, major questions remain over how reconstruction can be delivered effectively, transparently and equitably. The war has disproportionately affected many marginalised […]

item.title: We Owe Future Generations a Path Out of the Global Debt Crisis
item.description: Almost half of the world’s population now lives in countries that spend more money paying interest on their debts than on education or health. New data shows the cost of borrowing for African countries in particular rose 91% since 2020. Rising debt payments have reduced governments’ capacity to invest in children and build their human […]

item.title: Washington Fails to Block Cuba-Embargo Debate at the UN
item.description: The United States lost its campaign to scuttle a United Nations General Assembly debate on its decades-old embargo on Cuba as member states voted overwhelmingly against Washington to clear the rostrum for countries to deliberate on wide-ranging, more restrictive sanctions that have grounded Havana’s economy. Emotions ran high between the US and Cuba. Member states ... Read more
